_comment: Template for Claude Code. Copy this file to `.mcp.json` at the ROOT of your own project so Claude Code auto-loads it. The filename here deliberately omits the leading dot so this template is not itself auto-loaded when someone opens this directory in Claude Code. The key under mcpServers (`trino`) determines the tool prefix Claude Code sees: tools appear as mcp__trino__<name>. The trino-query sub-agent's `tools:` list must match that prefix. TRINO_USER / TRINO_PASSWORD are forwarded from the host shell via `-e VAR` (no `=value`), so secrets stay out of this file. TRINO_CATALOG / TRINO_SCHEMA are *fallback defaults for unqualified identifiers inside execute_query only* — they do NOT constrain the sub-agent: every discovery tool (list_schemas, list_tables, describe_table, show_create_table, show_stats) takes explicit catalog/schema arguments, and execute_query accepts fully-qualified SQL (`catalog.schema.table`) and `USE catalog.schema` statements. A single session can target multiple catalogs/schemas. Omit these two env vars entirely if you want to force the sub-agent to always qualify.
